title: Introduction to Machine Learning
text: Machine learning is a field of study that gives computers the ability to learn from data without being explicitly programmed. Common approaches include supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and reinforcement learning.

title: A Brief History of the Search Engine
text: Early search engines relied on simple keyword matching. Modern search engines use inverted indexes, ranking functions such as BM25, and machine learning to order results by relevance.

title: Cats and Dogs as Household Pets
text: Cats are independent animals that groom themselves and often prefer quiet spaces. Dogs are social animals that thrive on companionship and regular exercise with their owners.

title: Understanding Databases
text: A database index speeds up data retrieval at the cost of additional storage and slower writes. B-trees and hash indexes are two common index structures used by relational databases.

title: Neural Networks Explained
text: A neural network is composed of layers of interconnected nodes. Training a neural network involves adjusting weights through backpropagation to minimize a loss function on labeled data.

title: The Basics of Gardening
text: Healthy soil, consistent watering, and adequate sunlight are the foundations of a productive garden. Rotating crops each season helps prevent nutrient depletion and reduces pest pressure.

title: How Ranking Functions Work
text: Ranking functions such as term frequency inverse document frequency and BM25 estimate how relevant a document is to a query by combining term frequency with how rare a term is across the corpus.

title: Dogs in Working Roles
text: Dogs have been trained for centuries to herd livestock, guard property, and assist people with disabilities. Their strong sense of smell also makes them useful for search and rescue work.

title: An Overview of Distributed Systems
text: Distributed systems coordinate multiple independent computers to behave like a single coherent system. Consensus algorithms help these systems agree on shared state even when some nodes fail.

title: Cooking with Fresh Herbs
text: Fresh herbs such as basil, thyme, and cilantro add bright flavor to a dish when added near the end of cooking, since prolonged heat can destroy their delicate aromatic oils.
